title: Recap: What CLIP Does
concept: Callback to Unit 183: CLIP learns to place matching text and image pairs close together in a shared embedding space through contrastive training.

exercises.prompt: Quick recap: what does CLIP connect together?

options.text: Text and images in one shared space

exercises.explain: CLIP was trained to bring matching text and images close together in the same 'idea space'.

cards.title: Remember CLIP?
cards.body: Back in Unit 183, we met CLIP — the AI that learned to match pictures with words. Today we're bridging back to that idea before we build on it!

cards.title: What CLIP Does
cards.body: CLIP learns to place matching text and image pairs close together in a shared 'embedding space' — a map of meaning. It does this through contrastive training: pulling correct pairs close and pushing wrong pairs apart.

cards.title: A Magnetic Matchmaker
cards.body: Think of CLIP like a matchmaker with magnets. When a photo of a dog and the word 'dog' show up, CLIP pulls them together with a magnet. When a photo of a dog and the word 'car' show up, it pushes them apart instead.

cards.title: Dog Photo Meets Caption
cards.body: Show CLIP a photo of a dog and the caption 'a fluffy dog.' CLIP places their embeddings close together on its map. A wrong caption like 'a red bicycle' stays far away.

cards.title: Myth: CLIP Draws Pictures
cards.body: Myth: CLIP creates new images from text. Truth: CLIP doesn't generate anything — it only understands and matches. Other AIs (like image generators) use CLIP's matching skill to help them draw.

cards.title: Close vs. Far in Embedding Space
cards.body: CLIP's whole job is about distance in its idea-map: matching pairs end up close, mismatched pairs end up far.

left.text: Photo of a cat + text 'a cat' → placed close together

right.text: Photo of a cat + text 'a truck' → placed far apart

options.text: Placing matching text-image pairs close together in a shared space
Painting brand new artwork from scratch
Storing files on a hard drive

exercises.explain: CLIP's core skill is matching — it learns where text and images belong in a shared meaning space.

exercises.prompt: CLIP generates brand-new images from text prompts.
exercises.explain: False! CLIP only understands and matches text with images — it doesn't create new pictures itself.

exercises.sentence: CLIP uses ___ training to pull matching pairs close and push mismatched pairs apart.
exercises.explain: Contrastive training teaches CLIP to compare pairs — pulling correct matches close and pushing wrong matches away.

exercises.prompt: Which pair would CLIP place closest together in its embedding space?

options.text: A photo of a sunset + the caption 'a beautiful sunset'
A photo of a sunset + the caption 'a plate of pasta'
A photo of a sunset + a random string of numbers

exercises.explain: Since the caption truly matches the photo's content, CLIP would place these two embeddings very close together.

exercises.prompt: Boss Challenge: A photo of a beach and the text 'a sandy beach with waves' are fed into CLIP. What happens in CLIP's embedding space?

options.text: They are placed close together because they match
CLIP generates a new beach image
They are placed far apart since they are different formats

exercises.explain: Since the text accurately describes the image, CLIP's contrastive training pulls their embeddings close together in the shared space — that's the whole point of CLIP!
